---
title: "plotnine-polars"
jupyter: plotnine-polars
---

# Documentation and examples for `plotnine_polars`

This site collects examples and guides for the `plotnine_polars` package.

The focus here is slightly different from the original site:

- examples use Polars data frames directly
- plots are built with `df.ggplot()` where that reads naturally
- the fluent API comes from `plotnine_polars`, not from a fork of `plotnine`

Importing `plotnine_polars` registers the Polars dataframe namespace:

```{python}
import polars as pl
import plotnine_polars

df = pl.DataFrame({"x": [1, 2, 3], "y": [3, 2, 1]})

(
    df.ggplot()
    .aes("x", "y")
    .geom_point()
    .geom_line()
)
```

## Gallery adaptations

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Anscombe's Quartet](notebooks/anscombes-quartet.qmd)

Recreates Anscombe's classic quartet with `plotnine_polars`, `polars`, and
`scikit-learn`, including faceted regression lines and a polished final chart.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Please, I Want Some More Labels](notebooks/please-i-want-some-more-labels.qmd)

Shows how to add titles, subtitles, captions, and legend labels with
`plotnine_polars`.
:::
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [A Bar Plot With 2 Variables](notebooks/bar-plot-with-2-variables.qmd)

Builds a grouped bar chart step by step, using Polars for the data and
layer-specific label mappings for the dodged text annotations.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Facets (Subplots)](notebooks/facets.qmd)

Demonstrates `facet_wrap()`, `facet_grid()`, formula syntax, and free scales in
a compact subplot-focused notebook.
:::
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Citi Bike Trips](notebooks/citi-bike-trips.qmd)

Recreates the chapter 1 daily Citi Bike trips area chart with a compact,
pre-aggregated dataset and the `plotnine_polars` API.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [NYC Neighborhoods and Stations](notebooks/nyc-neighborhoods-and-stations.qmd)

Recreates the chapter 1 neighborhood-and-stations map with compact parquet
files and the `plotnine_polars` API.
:::
:::

## Guide adaptations

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Geometric Objects](notebooks/geometric-objects.qmd)

Condenses the plotnine geoms guide into a notebook covering points, bars,
rectangles, lines, ribbons, distributions, position adjustments, and summaries.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Aesthetic Mappings](notebooks/aesthetic-mappings.qmd)

Focuses on global versus layer-local `aes()`, literal settings, grouping, and
expression-based mappings.
:::
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### [Scales, Legends, and Guides](notebooks/scale-basics.qmd)

Covers manual scales, breaks, labels, limits, legend merging, guide placement,
and guide customization in the `plotnine_polars` style.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Source material

Most notebooks on this site are adapted from the official
[plotnine gallery](https://plotnine.org/gallery/) and
[plotnine guides](https://plotnine.org/guide/).
:::
:::
